task_completion.visual_evidence: An image processing application (e.g., ImageJ/Fiji, DS9) is open, displaying the 656nmos.fits and 502nmos.fits images.

task_completion.description: Create an irregular polygon selection around the 'Pillars of Creation'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A polygon selection tool is used to draw an irregular boundary around the central pillar structures in one of the images.

task_completion.description: Apply the identical polygon selection to both images.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The exact same polygon outline is visible on both the H-alpha and [OIII] images, or an ROI manager shows the region being applied to both windows.

task_completion.description: Measure the Area and Integrated Density for both images, ensuring the area is at least 20,000 pixels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A results table or measurement window displays 'Area' (with a value >= 20000) and 'Integrated Density' (or IntDen) for both the 656nmos and 502nmos images.

task_completion.description: Export the measurement results to roi_measurements.csv.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file manager, or terminal confirms the creation of 'roi_measurements.csv' in the ~/AstroImages/measurements/ directory containing the table data.

task_completion.description: Calculate the excitation ratio and save it to excitation_ratio.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A calculator, script, or spreadsheet is used to divide the H-alpha Integrated Density by the [OIII] Integrated Density, and a text editor or terminal shows the result being saved to 'excitation_ratio.txt' in ~/AstroImages/measurements/.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical image analysis tool to make the irregular polygon selection.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with an image viewer's GUI tools (like ImageJ/Fiji polygon tool or ROI manager) rather than just running a pre-written script that bypasses manual selection.

integrity.description: The exported CSV contains actual measurements derived from the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values in roi_measurements.csv match the values seen in the image software's results table.

integrity.description: The excitation ratio is genuinely calculated from the measured Integrated Densities.
integrity.visual_evidence: The value in excitation_ratio.txt mathematically corresponds to the H-alpha IntDen divided by the [OIII] IntDen shown in the measurement results, not a fabricated number.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required output CSV path is '/home/ga/AstroImages/measurements/roi_measurements.csv'. The output text file for the excitation ratio must be at '/home/ga/AstroImages/measurements/excitation_ratio.txt'. The minimum area for the region of interest must be at least 20000 pixels.
